ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a cashew nut food product class (FOODON_03301710) but no specific seaweed-flavoured cashew nut entry. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers cashew nuts as a base category. No specific seaweed-coated or flavoured cashew nut code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227493005 covers cashew nuts as a substance. No seaweed-flavoured variant exists in SNOMED CT; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve pack of seaweed cashew nuts weighs approximately 30 g, equivalent to a small snack bag as commonly sold at cafes and convenience stores in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cashew nuts, dry roasted, without salt added (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 12087); seaweed seasoning adjustment estimated from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods seaweed snack products
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy entry for dry-roasted cashew nuts (FDC ID 12087). Sodium and sugar values adjusted upward to reflect seaweed seasoning coating typical of commercial seaweed-flavoured cashew nut products, cross-referenced with USDA Branded Foods seaweed snack nut entries. Energy recalculated from adjusted macros. This is an estimated adaptation; no exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for seaweed cashew nuts.

gi_source.title: Cashew nuts, salted — GI value 22 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care, 2008)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 22 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for salted cashew nuts (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Seaweed-flavoured cashew nuts are not separately listed; the salted cashew nut GI is used as the closest analogous food. The seaweed coating adds minimal additional carbohydrate and is not expected to materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per 30 g serving calculated as (22 × 9.1 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 2.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from seaweed seasoning coating
hypertension.guidance: Seaweed cashew nuts contain approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g due to the seasoned coating; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small pack (30 g, ~126 mg sodium) and account for this within their daily sodium target of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 22) and high unsaturated fat and protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 22 and a glycemic load of approximately 2 per 30 g serving, seaweed cashew nuts have a minimal acute effect on postprandial blood glucose, making them a suitable occasional snack for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in controlled portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat with predominantly unsaturated fatty acids, but elevated sodium from seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cashew nuts provide heart-healthy monounsaturated fats (27.3 g/100 g) and are cholesterol-free, which is beneficial for cardiovascular health; however, the elevated sodium content from the seaweed seasoning (420 mg/100 g) warrants portion control for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Seaweed Cashew Nuts are a popular savoury snack consisting of whole roasted cashew nuts coated with a seasoned seaweed (nori) flavouring blend, typically including salt, sugar, and umami-rich seaweed powder. The product is widely sold in Singapore cafes, including Starbucks outlets, as well as in convenience stores and supermarkets across Southeast Asia. The cashew nuts are first dry-roasted to develop a rich, buttery crunch, then coated with a thin layer of seaweed seasoning that imparts a distinctive umami, slightly salty, and subtly oceanic flavour profile. The texture is crunchy with a savoury-sweet finish. Nutritionally, seaweed cashew nuts are energy-dense (approximately 574 kcal per 100 g), rich in monounsaturated fats (27.3 g/100 g), plant-based protein (15.3 g/100 g), and minerals including magnesium (260 mg/100 g), phosphorus (490 mg/100 g), iron (6 mg/100 g), and zinc (5.6 mg/100 g). The glycemic index is low (GI 22), making the snack suitable for moderate consumption by individuals managing blood glucose. The primary clinical consideration is sodium content (~420 mg/100 g) from the seaweed seasoning, which requires portion awareness for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. A standard single-serve pack is approximately 30 g. The product is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable, and is free of dairy. It may contain gluten depending on the seasoning formulation and should be treated as a tree-nut allergen food.
common_user_queries: How many calories in seaweed cashew nuts?
Is seaweed cashew nuts suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of cashew nuts?
Are seaweed cashew nuts high in sodium?
Can I eat seaweed cashew nuts if I have high blood pressure?
What nuts are sold at Starbucks Singapore?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for seaweed cashew nuts exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy dry-roasted cashew nuts (FDC ID 12087) with sodium and sugar adjusted to reflect seaweed seasoning, cross-referenced with USDA Branded Foods seaweed snack nut products. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (salted cashew nuts, GI 22) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 12087, dry-roasted cashew nuts without salt) with sodium adjusted to approximately 420 mg/100 g and sugar to 6.5 g/100 g to reflect typical seaweed seasoning coating, cross-referenced with USDA Branded Foods seaweed-flavoured nut products. Energy recalculated from adjusted macros using Atwater factors. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero — cashew nuts and seaweed seasoning contain no industrially produced trans fats per USDA FoodData Central data. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — cashew nuts are a plant food and contain no cholesterol. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for dry-roasted cashews. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0; naturally zero — this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for dry-roasted cashew nuts and is not expected to be present in meaningful quantities in this product. 7. GI of 22 is an analogous estimate based on salted cashew nuts from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008); no direct GI measurement exists for seaweed-flavoured cashew nuts. Glycemic load per 30 g serving = (22 × 9.06 g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 2.0. 8. Allergen status: contains tree nuts (cashew). May contain gluten depending on seasoning formulation; consumers with coeliac disease should check product label. 9. Halal status assumed based on typical ingredient composition; consumers should verify product-specific halal certification. 10. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact seaweed-flavoured cashew nut entries exist in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT. 11. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is sold in Singapore. 12. This food is sold at Starbucks One Fullerton, Singapore, per field survey data (GD001087).
